first_line_drugs.notes: Initial 4 hours IV fluids; bolus 10–20 ml/kg over 30–60 min only if shock; then continue deficit + maintenance over 36–48 hrs
Switch after initial 4 hours if available; continue rehydration over 36–48 hrs
Start after 1 hour of fluids at 0.05–0.1 U/kg/hr; no bolus; prepare 0.5–1 U/ml solution and infuse via pump
Add 40 mEq/L once urine output established and K+ normal; if hypokalemia use up to 80 mEq/L with ECG monitoring
Add when blood glucose <250 mg/dL; increase to D10 if glucose <150 mg/dL while acidosis persists

second_line_drugs.notes: Avoid routine use; consider only if severe acidosis (pH <6.9) with hemodynamic compromise
For suspected cerebral edema: Mannitol 0.5–1 g/kg or 3% saline 5 ml/kg IV

counselling: Child is critically ill and requires ICU care — बच्चा गंभीर रूप से बीमार है और ICU में इलाज जरूरी है
Insulin and fluids will correct the condition gradually — इंसुलिन और तरल पदार्थ से हालत धीरे-धीरे सुधरेगी
Frequent monitoring is required to prevent complications — जटिलताओं से बचने के लिए बार-बार जांच जरूरी है
Do not stop treatment early even if sugar improves — शुगर ठीक होने पर भी इलाज जल्दी बंद न करें
Long-term diabetes management will be needed — लंबे समय तक मधुमेह (डायबिटीज) का इलाज चलेगा
warning_signs: Sudden drop in heart rate or BP
referral_criteria: Refer to PICU/endocrinologist if severe DKA, altered sensorium, or complications
hospitalisation_criteria: All DKA cases require admission; ICU if moderate–severe DKA
notes: Correct dehydration slowly over 36–48 hrs. Avoid rapid fluid correction to prevent cerebral edema. Do not give insulin bolus.
source: ISPAD Guidelines 2022/2024, IAP Pediatric Endocrine Guidelines
guideline_changes: Lower insulin dose (0.05 U/kg/hr) preferred in young children to reduce cerebral edema risk
expected_course: Improvement in 24–48 hrs; full correction of acidosis within 36–48 hrs
key_clinical_points: Fluid therapy is first priority before insulin
Never give insulin bolus in pediatric DKA
